Details: Ultraviolet (UV) irradiation is effective for S. typhi inactivation. Two studies evaluating low-pressure UV lamps showed 1 to 5.5 log inactivation of S. typhi were achieved using UV doses ranging from 2.0 to 9.0 mWsec/cm2 [951, 952]. Another study tested S. typhi inactivation by a point-of-use water treatment system, which consisted of an activated carbon filter followed by a UV reactor [955]. The test system achieved >99.9999 percent (six log) S. typhi inactivation.
